title: Tranquil Malted Polish Barley Pilaf
description: A malty, aerated, nostalgic Polish dish featuring kosher salt, poached to perfection in a mason jar, this low-glycemic-friendly pilaf is a comforting side dish, it is also a great option for a light lunch
instructions: Rinse the pearl barley in a fine mesh strainer and drain well.
In a large bowl, whisk together the malted milk powder and kosher salt.
Add the water to the bowl and stir until the powder is dissolved.
Add the barley to the bowl and stir to combine.
Transfer the barley mixture to a mason jar and add the poached egg on top.
Dot the top of the egg with the butter.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the pilaf.
Cover the mason jar with a lid and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the pilaf chilled, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
